findings.justification: The profile link URL and user status string are concatenated without HTML escaping into $banned_profile_links, which is then interpolated as raw HTML into the admin-only suspicious-activity email template, allowing a user who controls their profile slug or status metadata to inject arbitrary HTML into the administrator notification email.

The banned-capabilities list is exposed via the public apply_filters('um_secure_register_form_banned_capabilities') hook, allowing any plugin or theme to silently remove capabilities from the list and bypass the registration-time admin-capability guard.

Raw POST form data from UM()->form()->post_form is stored unsanitized in the 'um_user_blocked__metadata' user meta field, which could expose sensitive submitted values or cause secondary injection issues if that meta is later displayed in an admin interface without escaping.

The current_user_can('manage_options') check inside revoke_caps() only determines the $user_agent label and does not gate the destructive operations (remove_all_caps, set_status, update_user_meta), so if any code path calls this method without its own authorization check the capability revocation runs unconditionally.
